title: eremophila glutinosa
intro: Eremophila glutinosa, also known as Sticky Emu Bush, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Eremophila glutinosa include its ability to soothe sore throats, calm digestive issues, and reduce fever. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
